title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs catch semantics, collection identity, randomness, and deterministic evidence from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
If probability is used, inject a narrow source, record seed or draw evidence, bound values, and keep policy separate from randomness.
Key owned entries by resolved stable ID while retaining display name and capture evidence without raw-input duplicates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 2105 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile deterministic catch decisions with stable identity and unchanged duplicate state; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
